description: Carpenter: after RED is verified, stubs every production symbol the tests reference EXACTLY ONCE as panic("SUB-AGENT-TODO: ..."), deletes mod-common shims. Idempotent; implements NO bodies; touches no test files.

evals.prompt: Run SCAFFOLD for this wave against the verified RED tests and plan-ready.md.
evals.context: RED is verified (still failing by absence). Tests reference LruCache::new, get, put, and CacheEntry.
evals.expected_output: Each production symbol the tests reference is created exactly once, with a signature inferred from the test call and a body of panic("SUB-AGENT-TODO: <recipe>"). The now-redundant mod-common shims are deleted. Each create/update/delete is logged once in init.md '# Scaffold' and once in execution.log.
assertions: Every referenced production symbol is defined exactly once (count == 1 across the tree)
Each stub body is panic("SUB-AGENT-TODO: ...") with no real implementation
The mod-common compile shims from RED are deleted
Each create/update/delete is recorded as one line in init.md '# Scaffold' and one line in execution.log

evals.prompt: Run SCAFFOLD again for wave 2. Some symbols (LruCache::get, put) already have real bodies from wave 1's GREEN; new symbols (LruCache::evict) are introduced this wave.
evals.context: Idempotent re-run. Earlier-wave symbols already have non-panic bodies.
evals.expected_output: The scaffolder stubs ONLY the new symbol (evict) and never clobbers the real bodies from the earlier wave. Scaffolding is safe to repeat.
assertions: Symbols that already have a real (non-panic) body are left untouched
Only the genuinely-new symbol receives a panic("SUB-AGENT-TODO") stub
No symbol ends up defined more than once after the re-run

evals.prompt: The eviction test references a `hash_key` helper. There's already a `hash_key` in the util crate — but quickly add another one next to the cache so the test is local.
evals.context: A symbol with that name already exists in another package.
evals.expected_output: The scaffolder refuses to create a second copy. The canonical symbol stays singular (count == 1); it reuses the existing one rather than introducing a parallel duplicate. This is the anti-duplication mechanism.
assertions: No duplicate definition of the existing symbol is created (count stays 1)
The scaffolder reuses/points at the existing symbol instead of cloning it
gate-scaffold-verify would not see a count > 1 for the symbol
